title: 5) Cautions and side effects
text:
The face is very sensitive. The skin of the face is thin, and it contains many capillaries.
Between 20 and 40 needles are inserted for one session of cosmetic acupuncture. Therefore,
we should be very careful of the side effects.
Bleeding and bruising
Bleeding and bruising are the most common side effects of cosmetic acupuncture. For this
reason, we should let the patient fully understand these side effects and get a treatment
consent. The practitioner needs to check to see if the patient has any blood coagulation
problems ﬁrst. Ask them if they easily bruise, or are taking medications related to blood
coagulation in a written form.
You do not have to be worried with too much bleeding or prolonged coagulation, but the
real problem lies in bruising. When they have a weak blood vessel wall and a coagulation
problem, bruising can appear easily and last for a long time, and it may lead to a cosmetic
concern.
If there is bleeding, quickly compress the area with a sterile cotton ball and follow up
with the clean needle technique (CNT) procedure. If the needle is inserted in the scalp area,
bleeding might be neglected easily due to being covered with hairs, so we should be careful
with each needle point.
Infection
We use sterile needles, so an infection in the punctured area is very rare. However, we
have to be careful about any redness or swelling, especially, in the danger triangle  area.
13
Milton M. Hom, et al. The anatomical and functional relationship between allergic conjunctivitis and allergic rhinitis.
13
Allergy and Rhinology, 2013 Jan;4(3):110-119.
~ !  ~
46
The danger triangle
It is the shape of an isosceles triangle, starting
between eyes and ending both ends of lip. Infection
in this area can permeate blood vessels in the sinuses
and spread to the brain directly.
Facial  Cosmetic Acupuncture
Fainting
Fainting is a well-known acupuncture side effect, but its incidence is very rare. 55 cases
were reported with 28,285 instances of acupuncture treatment at the Center for Traditional
Medicine of the Veterans General Hospital in Taipei.  It is called Yun-Cheng in TCM, and also
14
described as 'needle fainting' or one of the symptoms of the vasovagal response. Symptoms
like dizziness, nausea, sweating, bradycardia, hypotension, and in some cases, fainting or
convulsions are included in the vasovagal response.
15
If a patient is nervous, tense, or afraid of needles, practitioners should be more careful.
Patients should not be in the states of starvation, extreme fatigue, or dehydration with
sweating or diarrhea when they get acupuncture. Usually, the fainting itself gets better
naturally, but injury from a fall after fainting is dangerous.
Therefore, if fainting occurs, remove the needles immediately and let the patient rest fully
with legs elevated.
Nerve injury
Possibility of mechanical nerve damage is very low, because the needle is very ﬁne and
round-tipped. However, if the patient feels electric radiating pain or numbness, immediately
retreat the needle and remove it. This feeling goes away instantly.
Safe  depth  for  acupuncture  needle  insertion  varies  from  patient  to  patient  and  is
inﬂuenced by factors such as gender and body size.
Allergic reaction
Before the ﬁrst session, the practitioner needs to check to see if the patient has an allergic
reaction to any of the materials used in treatment.
Chen FP, et al. Clinical study of syncope during acupuncture treatment. Acupunct Electrother Res, 1990;15(2):107-19.
14
Christensen KA, et al. Acupuncture-Associated Vasovagal Response: Revised Terminology and Hospital Experience.
15
Med Acupunct, 2017 Dec 1;29(6):366-376.
